macro_summary: Thursday's session saw the S&P 500 slip 0.2% as hawkish FOMC minutes (per Axios, May 20) revealed a majority of officials back rate hikes if Hormuz-driven inflation persists, pushing the 10-year yield to 4.59%. WTI surged 3.6% to $101.78 on reports Iran is hardening its stance on a Strait of Hormuz ceasefire (per Reuters, May 21), while the EU ratified a 15% tariff cap deal with the US (per Greenwich Time, May 20). NVDA's record $81.6B quarter and $91B guide lifted SOXX 4.7% mid-week, but the broad watchlist remains heavily extended with 17 of 22 names hard-blocked on technicals.
commodities_note: WTI rebounded to $101.78 on renewed Hormuz tensions after a 6.6% weekly drop; gold held near $4,525; US10Y at 4.59% after FOMC minutes; VIX eased to 16.73 but geopolitical risk premium persists.

Headline R:R is 3.9:1 to $7.95, but the wide-stop R:R using structural support at $6.85 is only 1.07:1, below the 1.5 threshold. Fragility: ADR/China delisting risk — keep starter sizing. singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains structurally impaired by dual NIM compression channels — US10Y at 4.59% keeps USD funding costs high, and MAS S$NEER appreciation persists after the April policy hold. All three names are overbought (RSI 74-80) and +7.9-8.8% above their 50-day averages, rallying on declining volume without a fundamental catalyst. The next potential relief is the October MAS review; until then, no entry.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression persists, no relief catalyst before October.
singapore.key_development: All three banks extended further on low volume; DBS RSI hit 80 and OCBC 74, the most overbought readings since Q4 2025.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster continued to rally on NVDA's record quarter, but internal divergence is extreme: INTC and SK Hynix have outperformed by 46% and 23% over the month, while NVDA, ASML, AVGO, and TSM have lagged by 25-37%. Twelve of fourteen semis names are in CAUTION with extreme extension — only PLTR (valuation block) and CRWV (hard block) are not. The cluster is structurally bullish on AI capex, but tactically no entry points exist.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — wait for pullbacks.
semiconductors.key_development: NVDA's $81.6B quarter confirmed AI spending trajectory, but the semis cluster is priced for perfection with median vs_sma50 >10%.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster is mixed: MSFT upgraded to HOLD after slipping within 5% of its 50-day, while AMZN and GOOG remain +11-14% extended with no near-term entry. PLTR is in CAUTION on valuation grounds with a perfect 9/9 drawdown history, making it untouchable. The cluster is structurally positive on cloud and AI, but only MSFT is approaching an actionable level.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT nearly actionable; AMZN and GOOG extended; PLTR oversold on valuation.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's slide to 5% above the 50-day lifted the hard block, but tight upside at $424 keeps it in HOLD.

interconnected.name: Dell Technologies

interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if Dell’s AI server revenue accelerates post-NVDA partnership; watch for guidance raise at next earnings.

active_risks: Hormuz oil crisis: Iran hardening stance on ceasefire, Strait remains effectively closed, WTI at $101.78 (per Reuters, May 21).
Fed rate hike risk: FOMC minutes show majority of officials back hikes if inflation stays high; Warsh inherits hawkish mandate (per Axios, May 20).
US-China trade partial deal: China flags tariff cuts on ag goods, limited Boeing/beef deals reached; semiconductor export controls unchanged (per Reuters, May 20).
EU-US tariff cap deal ratified: 15% cap on most EU exports approved (per Greenwich Time, May 20).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $101.78 keeps energy costs elevated, defending PLTR and pressuring consumer names. Rate hike fears suppress tech multiples, keeping semis and BigTech clusters extended but without entry points. Singapore banks rallied on safe-haven flows but NIM compression remains unattractive.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: FOMC minutes hawkish (majority back hikes); Iran stance hardening on ceasefire, complicating Hormuz talks; EU tariff cap deal ratified. Oil up 3.6%.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries — 17 of 22 watchlist names are hard-blocked on technicals, and even those not blocked are overvalued or lack entry triggers. Focus on pre-earnings setups for AVGO and MSFT’s near-term re-rating potential.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 17 of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, with the remaining either overvalued or lacking entry triggers. The dominant risk is a Hormuz-driven inflation spike that forces a Fed rate hike, crushing tech valuations further.

watch_for_entry.note: R:R 3.9:1 to $7.95, but wide-stop to $6.85 gives 1.07:1 – below 1.5. Wait for test of $7.23 support with volume above 1M. ADR/China risk caps position size. PAPER TRADE.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
